manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna.
Your opponent's team words are: mango, dress, officer, speeding, crappie, slacks, sheep, turkey.
Distractor words are: sweatshirt, salmon, lieutenant, theft, larceny, parakeet, minnow.
The kill words are: nose.

TARGETS: melon, tuna, chef

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna.
Your opponent's team words are: mango, dress, officer, speeding, crappie, slacks, sheep, turkey.
Distractor words are: sweatshirt, salmon, lieutenant, theft, larceny, parakeet, minnow.
The kill words are: nose.

CLUE: food
TARGETS: melon, tuna, chef

The word salmon was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna.
Remaining words for your opponent are: mango, dress, officer, speeding, crappie, slacks, sheep, turkey.
Remaining distractor words are: sweatshirt, lieutenant, theft, larceny, parakeet, minnow.
Remaining kill words are: nose.

CLUE: red
TARGETS: robin, tuna, torso
message.reasoning:
Let me analyze the current situation:

My team words: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna
Opponent words: mango, dress, officer, speeding, crappie, slacks, sheep, turkey
Distractors: sweatshirt, lieutenant, theft, larceny, parakeet, minnow
Kill word: nose

My previous clue was "food" and related to melon, tuna, chef. The partner guessed "salmon" which was a distractor, so their turn ended.

Now I need to give another clue for my remaining words: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna

Wait, the remaining team words still show all the original words. That's odd - the melon, tuna, and chef should have been removed since they were my targets. Let me re-read the instructions... 

Actually, looking at it again - my clue "food" was supposed to be for melon, tuna, chef as targets. But my partner guessed "salmon" (a distractor), so their turn ended. This means they didn't successfully guess any of my team words. So all my team words should still be remaining.

Hmm, but that's confusing because typically in Codenames, when you give a clue and your team guesses correctly, those words are revealed/removed. But since they guessed wrong (salmon), none of my words were revealed.

Let me work with all my original team words still being in play:
torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna

I need to think of another single-word clue that relates to some of these. Let me think about groupings:

- torso, elephant, actor - could be related to "big" (torso is big, elephant is big, actor can be "big" in terms of star)
- pants, robin, tuna - not obvious
- elephant, pants, melon - could be "grey" or "gray"? Elephant is grey, pants could be grey, melon... not really grey


- actor, chef - both are professions
- melon, tuna - both are foods (I already used this)

I'll focus on finding a unique connection between these words. The challenge is creating a clue that relates to multiple words without being too obvious. I need something that bridges the remaining terms in an unexpected way.

I'm considering word associations that might link seemingly unrelated items. Maybe I'll explore categories or characteristics that could connect them in a surprising manner.

My strategy involves identifying potential hidden links: color, size, function, or abstract concepts. The goal is to craft a clue that feels clever and intrigues my partner to guess the correct words.

I'll start with color-based associations, noting which items share similar hues or visual qualities. Grey seems promising, with elephant and potentially pants connecting through shade.

I'm now considering professional roles as another angle, examining how different words might represent job categories or functions. This could reveal an interesting pattern of classification.

The key is finding an unexpected connection that makes my partner think, while still maintaining a logical relationship between the words I've selected.

Some intriguing word pairings emerge: robin and tuna share a "red" theme,actor and chef connect through performance, and pants and torso relate through clothing and body.

I'm exploring various semantic connections, seeking a clever way to group these seemingly disparate terms that will challenge and engage my partner's thinking.

The challenge is crafting a clue that's neither too obvious nor too obscure, balancing complexity with potential for accurate deduction.

nose, salmon, larceny, melon, lieutenant, elephant, officer, speeding, tuna, turkey, sweatshirt, robin, pants, crappie, chef, parakeet, mango, theft, slacks, minnow, rape, torso, sheep, actor, dress

nose, larceny, melon, lieutenant, elephant, officer, speeding, tuna, turkey, sweatshirt, robin, pants, crappie, chef, parakeet, theft, slacks, minnow, rape, torso, sheep, actor, dress

- sweatshirt - could be red in color
- pants - could be red
- dress - could be red
- tomato - not in the list
- blood - not in the list
- apple - not in the list

- nose (red nose)
- robin (robin's red breast)
- sweatshirt (red sweatshirt)
- pants (red pants)
- dress (red dress)
